preview_text:
Japan is the strong favourite, with the model assigning a 71% win probability against a Tunisia side rated at just 8%. The gap reflects a substantial quality differential in the model's estimation, leaving Tunisia with minimal chances of upset.

The draw emerges as the second-most-likely outcome at 21%, suggesting the match carries meaningful competitive balance despite Japan's dominance in outright-win odds. A Tunisia victory remains a low-probability event rather than a near-impossible one, indicating the model sees some path to an upset, however narrow. The W-D-L spread concentrates heavily on Japan but does not render the underdog entirely dismissible.

The market consensus rates Japan slightly lower than the model—about 8 percentage points—while pricing the draw and Tunisia win somewhat higher. Polymarket prices align closely with sportsbook consensus. These divergences are moderate; both sources agree on Japan as favourite but disagree on the magnitude of edge. The dashboard makes no claim as to which reading of the match is more accurate.
